app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.cyne: Chaotic
No cause-effect. Act first, novel practice required.
Clear
Known knowns. Best practices apply, cause-effect obvious.
Complex
Unknown unknowns. Safe-to-fail probes, emergent practice.
Complicated
Known unknowns. Expert analysis needed, multiple right answers.
Cynefin phase: {phase}
Classify the complexity of the operating environment.
Post-fire restoration with no baseline data and active erosion.
Emergency response training during active crisis with shifting needs.
Emergency solar deployment after natural disaster, no infrastructure baseline.
Waste crisis response with no existing infrastructure or baseline data.
Monoculture planting with established nursery stock and known survival rates.
Standardized curriculum with certified instructors and known pass rates.
Standard rooftop installation with proven equipment and trained installers.
Established collection routes with trained sorters and known buyer network.
Restoration of degraded land with unknown seed bank and variable rainfall.
Community-led learning with variable literacy and no prior baseline.
First off-grid deployment in region with unknown demand patterns.
New upcycling program with unknown community adoption patterns.
Multi-species agroforestry design requiring soil analysis and spacing models.
Custom training for multiple skill levels requiring needs assessment.
Grid-tied system requiring utility negotiations and custom inverter sizing.
Multi-stream sorting requiring material analysis and market research.
Cynefin Phase
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.desc: Provide context and goals for this assessment.
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.diag: Root-cause analysis of the challenge being addressed.
What land degradation, biodiversity loss, or agricultural challenge are you addressing?
What knowledge or skills gap exists? What are the consequences of not addressing it?
What energy access gap or infrastructure challenge are you addressing? What are the root causes?
What waste accumulation or management gap are you addressing? What are the root causes?
Describe the core challenge and its root causes...
e.g., Degraded pastureland from cattle overgrazing has reduced soil organic matter to <1%. Native species corridors are fragmented, limiting pollinator pathways...
e.g., Field operators lack standardized training on solar panel maintenance, leading to 35% system degradation in year 1. Knowledge transfer relies on informal peer learning...
e.g., Rural households in Sector 5 rely on diesel generators averaging 4 hours daily. High fuel cost ($12/week) limits productive electricity use and creates indoor health risks...
e.g., Unmanaged plastic waste accumulates along 2km of riverbank at 500kg/week. No formal collection infrastructure exists. Local informal recyclers recover <10% of recyclables...
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.sect: Define the challenge, outcomes, and complexity context for this assessment.
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.smar: e.g., Plant 200 native species seedlings across 5 hectares
e.g., Train 30 field operators to maintenance certification level
e.g., Generate 500 kWh/month from newly installed panels
e.g., Divert 2 tonnes of recyclable waste from landfill per month
Define measurable targets. Each outcome needs a description, metric, and target value.
At least one complete outcome is required

app.profile.help.faq.badgesAndIdentity.a: Badges mark Green Goods milestones like joining early, documenting your first submitted work, or supporting a garden. Your greengoods.eth name gives those badges a clearer identity.
app.profile.help.faq.badgesAndIdentity.q: How do badges and my Green Goods name connect?
app.profile.help.faq.dataStorage.answer: Drafts and media stay on this device until they upload. Approved work is stored as a public attestation so gardens can keep a durable record of impact.
app.profile.help.faq.duplicateHandling.a: Basic detection prevents obvious duplicates (for example, similar submissions within a short time window) to keep data clean.
app.profile.help.faq.gardensAndOperators: Gardens are the places or programs where impact happens. Gardeners document the work. Operators care for the garden record by reviewing submissions and approving the work that belongs there.
Choose the garden, pick the action you completed, add photos or notes, and submit. If your connection drops, Green Goods keeps the draft locally and sends it when you are back online.
How do I document garden work?
app.profile.help.faq.howToBringImpactOnc: You can bring impact onchain by creating a garden assessment and submitting it to the Green Goods EAS.
How can I bring impact onchain?
app.profile.help.faq.howToContact.answer: Telegram is the fastest path for help from the Green Goods community. You can also use the onboarding form if you want to share more context about your garden.
app.profile.help.faq.howToGetInvolved.an: Open Gardens, choose one that matches your work, and join as a gardener. After you join, the garden appears on your profile and you can start documenting work for it.
app.profile.help.faq.howToGetInvolved.qu: How do I join a garden?
app.profile.help.faq.howToLearnMore.answ: You can learn more about Green Goods by visiting our website, joining our Discord server, and following us on social media.
app.profile.help.faq.howToLearnMore.ques: How can I learn more about Green Goods?
app.profile.help.faq.offlineSupport.answ: Yes. Drafts stay on your device while you are offline, including photos and notes. When you reconnect, Green Goods uploads the saved work so you can keep moving in the field.
app.profile.help.faq.offlineSupport.ques: Can I use the app offline?
app.profile.help.faq.reviewAndApproval.a: An operator checks whether the work fits the garden and has enough evidence. Approved work becomes part of the garden record; rejected work can be revised or clarified.
app.profile.help.faq.reviewAndApproval.q: What happens after I submit work?
app.profile.help.faq.smartAccountAddress: Your profile shows a smart account address used for submissions and approvals. You can also link your regular wallet (EOA).
What is my smart account address?
app.profile.help.faq.whatIsEAS.answer: EAS is the public record Green Goods uses after work is approved. It creates a verifiable receipt of what was documented and who approved it, without making you manage the technical details.
app.profile.help.faq.whatIsEAS.question: What does attestation mean here?
app.profile.help.faq.whatIsGreenGoods.an: Green Goods helps communities document regenerative work, connect it to a garden, and turn approved evidence into a shared impact record.
app.profile.help.faq.whatIsImpact.answer: Impact refers to measurable positive environmental outcomes — such as planting trees, restoring soil, managing waste, or educating communities. Green Goods tracks these contributions across four domains: Solar, Agro, Edu, and Waste.
app.profile.help.faq.whoCanSubmitWork.an: Anyone who has joined a garden as a gardener can submit work. You do not need to be technical; you just need clear evidence of what happened.

public.glossary.canonicalBody: The docs glossary is the source of truth for vocabulary across code, copy, and design prompts. Builder terms (Allowlist, Bundler, ERC-4337) live there too.
public.glossary.heroLede: Plain-language definitions for the words you'll meet here. Click any term to read the canonical entry in the docs glossary.
public.glossary.lede: Each definition is a starting line. The docs glossary holds the full entry, with cross-references and technical detail for builders.
public.glossary.term.action.body: A documented activity a gardener can perform — the unit of work template. Each Action names what to do, what to capture, and what proof comes next.
public.glossary.term.action.label: Action
public.glossary.term.assessment.body: The diagnosis-and-plan stage of a Garden's season — written by operators and evaluators to name what the place needs and what counts as good.
public.glossary.term.assessment.label: Assessment
public.glossary.term.cookieJar.body: A shared fund where supporters donate to a Garden's near-term Work. Cookie Jars are allowlist-gated so the right hands can claim from them.
public.glossary.term.cookieJar.label: Cookie Jar
public.glossary.term.evaluator.body: A domain expert who reviews submitted Work and signs off with a confidence band and verification method. Their care is what turns a field log into evidence.
public.glossary.term.evaluator.label: Evaluator
public.glossary.term.garden.body: A community of gardeners doing regenerative work in a place. Each Garden has its own Vault, operator, and gardeners, and its own public record.
public.glossary.term.garden.label: Garden
public.glossary.term.gardener.body: A person who documents Work in the field — soil turned, seedlings planted, hours given — using the Green Goods app, even offline.
public.glossary.term.gardener.label: Gardener
public.glossary.term.impactCertificate.b: A bundle of the season's approved Work, evaluator-verified and anchored to a public blockchain so the record stays readable beyond any one platform.
public.glossary.term.operator.body: The person who runs a Garden — assembling the season's plan, accepting gardeners, and confirming the Work that gets recorded.
public.glossary.term.operator.label: Operator
public.glossary.term.vault.body: A long-term deposit attached to a Garden whose yield supports the Garden's Work over many seasons. Endowing a Vault keeps the principal in place.
public.glossary.term.vault.label: Vault
public.glossary.term.work.body: A specific instance of an Action performed by a gardener — captured with photo, description, and metadata, then attested on-chain after operator approval.
public.glossary.term.work.label: Work
public.glossary.title: Words the public record uses.

public.home.hero.lede: Green Goods makes regenerative work easier to support across solar, agroforestry, education, and waste, turning everyday contributions into a trusted public record of how places grow healthier together.
public.home.hero.tagline: From good intentions to green outcomes
public.home.hero.title: From <accent>good</accent> intentions to <noBreak><accent>green</accent> outcomes</noBreak>.
public.home.install.description: Install the Green Goods app to log Work, capture evidence, and follow Gardens you support, even offline.
public.home.install.title: Bring the field with you
public.home.loop.assessBody: A Garden gathers gardeners, operators, evaluators, and (ideally) funders around a real place. Together they read where it is and where it's meant to go.
public.home.loop.fieldGuide: Browse the field guide of regenerative Actions
public.home.loop.fieldGuideKicker: Curious how the work gets planned?
public.home.loop.fundBody: A Garden's community and funders give directly today, or commit a deposit so yield supports the Work over time.
public.home.loop.title: Four steps. Repeated, <line2>season after season</line2>
public.home.loop.verifyBody: Operators bundle the approved Work into an Impact Certificate. Evaluators, from many backgrounds and not only topical experts, then verify what the certificate claims.
public.home.loop.workBody: Gardeners submit Work from the field with media, details, and metadata. Operators review those submissions before they become part of the public record.
public.home.personas.community.body: A garden is held by the people around it — neighbors who visit on a Saturday, elders who mentor a first season, friends who share the season's story. Community shows up in the places where the work happens, and the public record is for them too.
public.home.personas.community.name: Community
public.home.personas.community.role: Show up locally — visit, witness, mentor, share the season's story.
public.home.personas.evaluator.body: Evaluators are the trust layer of the public record — domain experts who review submitted work, sign off with a confidence band, and name the verification method behind each approval. Their care is what turns a field log into evidence.
public.home.personas.evaluator.name: Evaluators
public.home.personas.evaluator.role: Verify the record — review submitted work, sign off on confidence and method.
public.home.personas.funder.body: Funders make seasons possible — by donating directly to a Garden's shared fund, by endowing a vault whose yield supports many seasons, or by holding an Impact Certificate as a record of regenerative outcomes. Each path lands with a Garden, not a platform.
public.home.personas.funder.name: Funders
public.home.personas.funder.role: Resource the work — fund a vault, sponsor an action, hold an Impact Certificate.
public.home.personas.gardener.body: A gardener walks a place every season — mapping soil, planting trees, capturing what was done. The Green Goods app turns those moments into a public record without taking the gardener out of the field.
public.home.personas.gardener.name: Gardeners
public.home.personas.gardener.role: Document the work — soil turned, seedlings planted, hours given.
public.home.personas.lede: Healthy places grow when many hands and eyes meet around the same record. Five people show up here, each making the proof true in their own way.
public.home.personas.operator.body: Operators are the steady hand of a garden — the one who calls the season's plan, who decides what counts, who approves a gardener's first work. They are the connective tissue between a place's intentions and its proof.
public.home.personas.operator.name: Operators
public.home.personas.operator.role: Run the garden — assemble the season's plan, accept gardeners, confirm the work.
public.home.personas.title: It takes more than gardeners.
public.home.proof.assessmentsNote: Independent evaluator confirmations, anchored to a public reference.
